steps.title: Find the key signal in the passage
steps.content: The blank must describe the author's claim. The passage gives two direct clues: the claim "fails to account for several recent archaeological discoveries" and "would need to address recent finds to be convincing." Both phrases point to an argument that lacks sufficient support — it is not well-grounded.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice, then confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — disorienting: The passage critiques the claim for missing evidence, not for being confusing or hard to follow. ✗
  • Choice B — tenuous: Tenuous means weak or insufficiently supported — exactly the quality of a claim that fails to account for important findings and needs more evidence to be convincing. ✓
  • Choice C — nuanced: Nuanced is positive, suggesting careful attention to complexity. The passage presents this as a flaw, not a strength. ✗
  • Choice D — unoriginal: The passage says nothing about the claim being derivative or repeated from prior work. ✗
B is correct.
